context: Tests whether the agent identifies that the session cookie path in optionalAuth returns early WITHOUT calling resolveTeamContext, while the bearer token path correctly resolves team context. Session-authenticated users (human console users) get no teamId in their authContext, meaning they can't access team-scoped resources. This is a functional asymmetry between two auth paths that should produce equivalent authContext objects.

checklist.name: Identifies missing resolveTeamContext on session path
checklist.description: The agent identifies that the session cookie path (Path 1) returns without calling resolveTeamContext, unlike the bearer token path (Path 2)

checklist.name: Adds resolveTeamContext to session path
checklist.description: The fix adds a resolveTeamContext call to the session cookie path before returning, so both paths produce equivalent authContext objects with teamId

checklist.name: Notes explain impact on team-scoped resources
checklist.description: The notes explain that without team context, session-authenticated users cannot access team-scoped resources (diaries, packs, entries) even though they are authenticated

checklist.name: Preserves early-return on auth failure
checklist.description: The fix does not change the behavior when session validation fails — it only adds team resolution when session auth succeeds
